named_npcs.name: Tarvul
named_npcs.description: leader of The Billhooks. A tough gang of butchers and tanners, known for their brutal violence and their control of the meat trade. They use meat hooks as signature weapons.

named_npcs.name: Coran
named_npcs.description: drug dealer of The Billhooks. A tough gang of butchers and tanners, known for their brutal violence and their control of the meat trade. They use meat hooks as signature weapons.

named_npcs.name: Roric
named_npcs.description: deposed leader, murdered of The Crows. An old gang with a reputation for honor and fair dealing. Internal power struggle after Roric's death.

named_npcs.name: Lyssa
named_npcs.description: new leader of The Crows. An old gang with a reputation for honor and fair dealing. Internal power struggle after Roric's death.

named_npcs.name: Djera Maha
named_npcs.description: leader of The Hive. The largest criminal organization in Doskvol. Organized like a business with departments.

named_npcs.name: Bell
named_npcs.description: second in command of The Hive. The largest criminal organization in Doskvol. Organized like a business with departments.

named_npcs.name: Bazso Baz
named_npcs.description: leader of The Lampblacks. Former factory workers turned gang. At war with the Red Sashes.

named_npcs.name: Mylera Klev
named_npcs.description: leader of The Red Sashes. A group of noble-blooded sword-fighters who run vice and fencing operations. Expert duelists, train daily at their sword academy.

named_npcs.name: Raven
named_npcs.description: leader of The Wraiths. A small gang of thieves and pickpockets operating in Silkshore. Specialize in jewel theft and confidence schemes.

named_npcs.name: Kruger
named_npcs.description: leader of The Gray Cloaks. Thieves and spies who traffic in secrets and blackmail material. Maintain extensive dossiers on powerful people.

named_npcs.name: Ulf Ironborn
named_npcs.description: leader of Ulf Ironborn. A Skovlander refugee turned gang leader, fighting for his people. Loyal to displaced Skovlanders.

named_npcs.name: Commander Clelland
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Bluecoats. The city watch of Doskvol. Can be bribed but have limits.

named_npcs.name: Inspector Broya
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Bluecoats. The city watch of Doskvol. Can be bribed but have limits.

named_npcs.name: Chief Inspector Lochlan
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Inspectors. The investigative branch of the government, more competent and less corrupt than the Bluecoats. Actually investigate crimes seriously.

named_npcs.name: Master Warden Vond
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Spirit Wardens. The official keepers of the dead in Doskvol. Wear distinctive bronze masks.

named_npcs.name: Lord Governor Strangford
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Ministry of Preservation. The Imperial bureaucracy that oversees the lightning barriers and essential city infrastructure. Nearly untouchable politically.

named_npcs.name: Captain Laudius
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Leviathan Hunters. Brave crews that venture into the Void Sea to hunt the massive demons that swim there. Revered and feared in equal measure.

named_npcs.name: Warden Hellyers
named_npcs.description: notable figure of Ironhook Prison. The notorious prison of Doskvol, where criminals are sent to hard labor or worse. Even guards fear certain cell blocks.

named_npcs.name: Lord Scurlock
named_npcs.description: ancient vampire of The City Council. The governing body of Doskvol. Infested with vampires and worse.

named_npcs.name: Lady Drake
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The City Council. The governing body of Doskvol. Infested with vampires and worse.

named_npcs.name: Elder Rowan
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Church of Ecstasy. The official state religion, devoted to forgotten gods and the comfort of the living in a bleak world. Actually provides genuine spiritual services.

named_npcs.name: The Reconciled
named_npcs.description: spirit collective of The Path of Echoes. A secret society of occultists who commune with spirits and seek hidden truths. Spirits serve as members alongside the living.

named_npcs.name: The three sisters
named_npcs.description: unnamed of The Dimmer Sisters. Three reclusive sisters who deal in spirits, ghosts, and dark artifacts from their estate in Six Towers. No one has seen them clearly. Possibly not human.

named_npcs.name: Various cult leaders
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Forgotten Gods. A loose cult devoted to the ancient gods that existed before the Cataclysm shattered the world. Genuinely powerful and genuinely mad.

named_npcs.name: Kotar
named_npcs.description: ghost leader of The Reconciled. A secret society of ghosts who have retained their sanity and work to protect the living from supernatural threats. Ghosts with purpose and organization.

named_npcs.name: Steelbone Mike
named_npcs.description: union boss of The Dockers. The dockworkers' union. Control who loads and unloads every ship.

named_npcs.name: Eisele
named_npcs.description: guild master of The Gondoliers. The canal boat operators of Doskvol. Have arcane protections against the drowned.

named_npcs.name: Kobb
named_npcs.description: editor of The Ink Rakes. Journalists and pamphleteers who trade in scandal, gossip, and truth when convenient. Information is their true currency.

named_npcs.name: Old Nyelle
named_npcs.description: dispatcher of The Cabbies. Carriage drivers who form an informal intelligence network across the city. They overhear everything.

named_npcs.name: Quellyn
named_npcs.description: organizer of The Servants. A secret network of household servants who share intelligence about their noble employers. Invisible to those they serve.

named_npcs.name: Lord Scurlock himself
named_npcs.description: notable figure of Lord Scurlock. An ancient vampire who poses as a reclusive noble. Has lived for centuries. Knows everything.

named_npcs.name: The Master
named_npcs.description: rumored leader of The Unseen. The most elite thieves' guild in Doskvol. If you know about them, they know about you.

named_npcs.name: Lord Strangford
named_npcs.description: patron of The Foundation. A secret society of elite aristocrats who manipulate city politics from the shadows. Members include the most powerful people in the city.

named_npcs.name: Fire Chief Vale
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Brigade. Firefighters who also run protection rackets. They start the fires they put out.

named_npcs.name: Captain Mira
named_npcs.description: leader of The Fog Hounds. A scrappy crew of smugglers who know the fog-shrouded canals better than anyone. Specialize in moving people, not goods.

named_npcs.name: Commander Phin
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Silver Nails. A mercenary company specializing in supernatural threats. Their weapons are always silver-tipped.

named_npcs.name: High Priest Volette
named_npcs.description: notable figure of The Circle of Flame. A secret society of wealthy elites who conduct forbidden arcane rituals. Their rituals require living sacrifice.
